sourceStories.sourceTitle: City Students Build Solar Bus Stop Lights
sourceStories.sourceSummary: A student engineering club partnered with the city to install small solar lights at bus stops so riders can read route signs after dark.
A public library launched a free Saturday program where teens and adults practice conversational English and Spanish in guided groups.

sourceStories.sourceTitle: River Cleanup Team Tracks Plastic Waste
sourceStories.sourceSummary: Volunteers used a new tracking app during a cleanup day to measure the amount and type of plastic collected near local riverbanks.

sourceStories.sourceTitle: High School Adds Quiet Study Courtyard
sourceStories.sourceSummary: After student feedback, a high school converted an unused courtyard into a quiet reading and study zone with shade and seating.

sourceStories.sourceTitle: Teen Robotics Team Wins Regional Match
sourceStories.sourceSummary: A local robotics team won a regional competition by designing a robot arm that sorted objects faster and with fewer errors.
Community volunteers expanded a garden project and doubled the number of fresh produce boxes donated to nearby families this month.

sourceStories.sourceTitle: Transit App Adds Live Elevator Alerts
sourceStories.sourceSummary: A city transit app now sends live alerts when station elevators are out of service to help riders plan accessible routes.

sourceStories.sourceTitle: Museum Offers Student Audio Guide Challenge
sourceStories.sourceSummary: A science museum invited students to script short audio guides for exhibits, and winning entries will be recorded for summer visitors.
Parks staff tested shaded cooling stations and water refill points after a warm spring week to improve safety during outdoor events.

sourceStories.sourceTitle: Students Launch School Podcast on Local History
sourceStories.sourceSummary: Middle and high school students collaborated on a podcast series featuring interviews with longtime residents about neighborhood history.
